title: Chile Copper Output Falls for Fourth Straight Month as Strike Action Persists
description: Chile Copper Output Falls for Fourth Straight Month as Strike Action Persists Bloomberg
htmlContent:
Chile Copper Output Falls for Fourth Straight Month as Strike Action Persists

By Andrea Schiavocampo, Bloomberg • June 5, 2026

SANTIAGO – Chilean copper production declined for the fourth consecutive month in May 2026, as prolonged industrial action at two of the country's largest mines continued to curtail output, adding to supply pressures that have already pushed the global copper market into a significant deficit.

National copper commission Cochilco reported that May output fell 6.3% year-on-year to 460,000 tonnes, the lowest monthly figure since early 2023. Workers at BHP's Escondida mine and Glencore's Collahuasi operation have been engaged in work-to-rule actions and intermittent stoppages since February, following the breakdown of annual wage negotiations.

The two mines together account for approximately 8% of global copper supply. Analysts at Capital Economics said the prolonged disruptions were "adding meaningful upside risk to copper prices already strained by surging AI and electrification demand."

State copper giant Codelco, which produces roughly 10% of the world's copper, also reported that output at its flagship Chuquicamata division was below target in May due to unplanned maintenance. The company has been battling declining ore grades at several of its aging operations, an issue it aims to address through a multi-billion dollar modernisation programme that is running behind schedule.

On the London Metal Exchange, three-month copper remained firm above $10,800 per tonne, close to its highest level since March. Inventories on exchange fell to 84,700 tonnes – barely a day of global consumption – underscoring the tightness in physical markets.

"The supply side of the copper equation is deteriorating at precisely the moment demand is accelerating," said Natasha Morrow, commodities strategist at Standard Chartered. "We see this deficit widening materially through the second half of 2026."

Chile's government has urged both sides in the Escondida and Collahuasi disputes to return to mediated negotiations, but no date has been set for fresh talks.

Tags: copper, Chile, Escondida, Collahuasi, Codelco, supply disruption, strike, mining

Sentiment signal: bearish supply / bullish price
